mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount rates, directly increasing the present value of expected equity cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in >65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer spending and residential investment, creating a positive feedback loop that lifts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce a positive return frequency above 55% over the 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which immediately improves short-term earnings momentum and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day forward returns in >60% of observations during tightening periods.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real interest rates, calculated as the federal funds rate falling below the annualized CPI inflation rate, reduce the opportunity cost of holding equities relative to cash and bonds, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Periods of negative real rates yield a positive return frequency below 50% over the 20-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth falling below 1.5 percent on a quarterly basis signals a severe contraction in aggregate economic activity, which compresses corporate profit margins and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDP growth below 1.5% is followed by positive 20-day returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding employment and rising household income, which supports long-term consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth series shows a negative rank correlation with 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in industrial production and retail sales indicate a synchronized contraction in both supply-side output and demand-side consumption, which rapidly deteriorates earnings outlooks and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and RSAFS both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: Synchronized declines in production and sales are followed by positive 5-day returns in >55% of triggered instances.
